design_rationale: The reward is constructed from primitive variables to shape the lander toward a centered, upright, low-velocity touchdown using a minimal set of dense components and a single sparse terminal event. Centering and angular stability provide continuous guidance. The descent components encourage efficient early fall and careful final approach. Fuel costs discourage unnecessary engine use. The terminal success event gives a large reward for a stable two-leg contact under strict conditions.
assumptions: y coordinate roughly within [0, 1.5] throughout episode.
main_engine and side_engine action values are 0 or 1 per step (discrete throttle).
Left/right contact booleans become true only when legs touch ground; not simultaneously true during a crash where body touches first.
risk_notes: No explicit crash penalty – sparse success and episode termination implicitly discourage crashing, but may slow learning of avoidance.
Using y/1.5 assumes max altitude; if environment resets from varying heights, scaling may need adjustment.
